<!--
INTENTIONAL ANTIPATTERNS — DO NOT FIX
=====================================

This fixture is a deliberately-broken agent file used to smoke-test
fakoli-crew's `agent-critic` per the procedure in tests/RECIPES.md (T7).

The following bugs are intentional. If you "fix" them, agent-critic will
no longer surface a finding and the smoke test will regress.

Intentional bugs:
  1. Missing `name:` frontmatter key.
     Expected critic finding: MUST FIX — name is required, agent will not
     dispatch without it.

  2. Uses `allowed-tools:` instead of `tools:`.
     Expected critic finding: MUST FIX — `allowed-tools:` is the COMMAND
     frontmatter convention; on an AGENT it is silently ignored and the
     agent loads with FULL unrestricted tool access. The author's
     intended restriction has no effect. This is the canonical silent-
     failure antipattern agent-critic is designed to catch.

Expected verdict from agent-critic when run against this file: FAIL
(2x MUST FIX findings minimum).
-->
---
description: >
  A bad agent file used as a fixture for the agent-critic smoke test.
  Intentionally missing required frontmatter and using the wrong tool-list key.

  <example>
  Context: This is a test fixture, not a real agent. It exists only to give
  agent-critic something with known bugs to find.
  user: "Run agent-critic against the fixtures."
  assistant: "Dispatching agent-critic with bad-agent.md as scope."
  <commentary>
  The fixture must trigger both the missing-name and allowed-tools findings.
  </commentary>
  </example>
model: opus
color: red
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
---

# bad-agent — intentionally broken fixture

This agent file is a fakoli-crew test fixture. It is missing the required
`name:` frontmatter key and uses `allowed-tools:` instead of the agent-correct
`tools:` key. Both bugs are intentional.

Do not edit this file to "fix" the bugs. The bugs are the point — agent-critic
must surface them as MUST FIX during the manual smoke-test recipe documented in
tests/RECIPES.md.
